titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Noriaki Tsuchimoto Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Kohei Yamaguchi, Haruko Nakamura, and Alison Clemens

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid written in English and Japanese.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday October 7, 2020 at 07:25

did.unittitle: Noriaki Tsuchimoto papers

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Noriaki Tsuchimoto's papers related to Japanese films, especially the documentary genre and social issues in Japan and the world. Noriaki Tsuchimoto (1928-2008) was one of the most prominent documentary film directors in Japan. The collection consists of various types and formats including his diaries, correspondence, books, film scripts, and photographs.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from the Librarian for Japanese Studies, 2020.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Noriaki Tsuchimoto (1928-2008), Japanese documentary film director, known for his films on Minamata disease and modernization on Asia.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The papers are arranged in four series: I. Correspondence, 1952-2017. II. Biographical, 1940-2012. III. Film Projects, 1927-2013. IV. Collected Ephemera, 1938-2015.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research. Materials containing Minimata patient information are restricted until January 1, 2046.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright is retained by the creator(s) of this collection for materials they have authored or otherwise produced. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Noriaki Tsuchimoto Papers (MS 2115). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Acquired by the Japanese Studies Librarian from Motoko Tsuchimoto.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Titles of items were provided by Noriaki Tsuchimoto's wife, Motoko Tsuchimoto. Items which were not given a title have been assigned one based on the contents and indicated with square brackets. Inventory information created by Motoko Tsuchimoto is available in the collection.

did.unittitle: Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I contains the correspondence of Noriaki Tsuchimoto. Additional correspondence items can be found in the Film Projects and Collected Ephemera series, as they reference specific projects or events in Tsuchimoto's life.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is arranged into 5 subseries: General, Shokan hikae, Hagaki postcards, Meishi business cards, and Address books.

Film Projects

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III includes script books, filming cut logs, research and reference materials used for creating the various films, photographs from filming and research, and schedules for filming with notes on activities and ideas. Titles not italicized are project topics and not movie titles. Film reels and other related photos are included in the 'Film' and 'Photographs' series of the collection, respectively.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III is arranged by film title. The Minamata film project was Tsuchimoto's most extensive project and has therefore been placed at the beginning of the series.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This grouping is arranged based on the corresponding films for which they were produced and are listed in chronological order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A collection of scriptbooks for movies and projects related to topic of "tetsu" (steel). Film projects include: 「日本の鉄鋼」（Nihon no Tekko),「製鉄所」（Seitetsujo),「鉄」 (Tetsu) ,「新しい鉄」（Atarashi Tetsu),「鉄をつくる」（Tetsu o Tsukuru）,「製鉄の近代化」（Seitetsu no Kindaika),「海に築く製鉄所」（Umi ni Kizuku Seitetsujo).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The computer files are unprocessed and may contain sensitive information or be in a physical state that would prohibit use. Researchers wishing to request access should email mssa.assist@yale.edu. The request should outline the scope and purpose of the research project, why the researcher believes the material is relevant to their project, and contact information. The review may take several weeks.
